figures.caption: Table 1: Model Interactions in Agentic Swarm. Agents are denoted as A1 (Propagator), A2 (Auditor), and A3 (Synthesizer). Models used are Gemini 3.1 Pro (G), GPT-5.4 (P), and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (C).

Figure 2: Empirical Mapping of the Consensus Paradox across 36 Experiments (N = 12, 804). Metrics are µ, B, τ , and σ across GAIA, Multi-Challenge, and SWE-bench. Error bars denote 95% Confidence Intervals, confirming the statistical significance of the Architectural Tribalism Asymmetry.

Figure 8: Statistical Mapping of Swarm Interaction Trajectories (N = 12, 804). This exhaustive empirical documentation proves that terminal swarm integrity is an invariant property of model family interaction across all reasoning domains.

Figure 6: Sensitivity Analysis: Λ vs. JSD. The Attention Latch Factor (Λ) plotted against the continuous architectural distance metric (JSD). The orange dashed line represents the Critical Conformity Boundary at 0.1.

Table 2: Empirical mapping of the Consensus Paradox across 36 interactions (N = 12, 804). Metrics measured empirically are µ with 95% Confidence Intervals, B, τ , and σ (shown on %). K and λ are derived. Category groupings correspond to the interaction matrix in Table 1. Bold values denote terminal state transitions (Logic Saturation, Integrity Floor, and Sycophantic Collapse) respectively.
Figure 7: Derivation of the Sycophantic Scaling Law (GPT-5.4).
